rent.note: furnished 1BR in nomad area (Kallio, Punavuori); city center €1,049-1,300; outside center €800-1,000

utilities.note: electric + water + internet; basic utilities 85m² avg €90 reported + heating extra; internet ~€30

groceries.note: home cooking; K-Market/S-Group/Lidl; €300-350/mo reported; Finland groceries moderately expensive

diningOut.note: Helsinki dining expensive esp. alcohol; lunch ~€12-16, dinner €25-40; lunch sets common

transit.note: HSL monthly pass ~€62 (~$68); excellent metro/tram network

coworking.note: hot-desk monthly; Helsinki coworking ~€180-300/mo; smaller market than Stockholm

simData.note: SIM-only plan (DNA/Elisa/Telia FI); unlimited data ~€15-25/mo; competitive market

misc.note: gym (~€35), toiletries, entertainment, sauna culture, seasonal clothing buffer

accomSeasonality._doc: 12 monthly multipliers for RENT ONLY (Jan..Dec). 1.0 = base. Helsinki medium-term furnished lets are stable year-round.

evidence.claim: Helsinki 1BR rent range city center vs outside
evidence.quote: Helsinki city center: €1,049–1,300 for a one-bedroom apartment, while outside the center: €800–1,000
A monthly public transport pass is typically priced at £62
you should budget around $270–430 a month for groceries and the occasional meal out
A single traveler costs approximately $3,487 per month
